subject: 5 ADD categories (del/dex/uns/bod/xbd)
source_doctrine: Each is 'one Add (element of difficulty)'. The 5 categories are: DELAY DEXTERITY UNUSUAL SURFACE BODY CROSS BODY
modern_doctrine: Modern operational notation uses these exact 5 bracketed flag tokens
notes: FOUNDATIONAL DOCTRINE ALIGNED across 23 years. The 5-category taxonomy directly maps to operational notation flag vocabulary

subject: flag-count-equals-ADD principle
source_doctrine: 'Each of these is equal to one Add' — each bracketed flag = 1 ADD; total ADD = total flag count
modern_doctrine: Modern multiplier doctrine uses modifier-stack math which is pedagogically derived
notes: CRITICAL: Source says flag-count IS the ADD math. Modern multiplier doctrine is a pedagogical representation that should mathematically equal flag count when correctly modeled. When the two diverge the flag count is canonical per this source

subject: Ducking and Diving distinction
source_doctrine: Ducking = head goes under THEN trick on OTHER side. Diving = head goes under THEN trick on SAME side.
modern_doctrine: Modern: ducking and diving both +1 body modifiers; distinction not explicit in modifier table
notes: Modern modifier table treats them as functionally equivalent +1/+1 body modifiers; source's 'other side vs same side' distinction is structural but not preserved in modifier values

subject: Paradox = body category ADD
source_doctrine: Source: paradox 'adds a level of difficulty in the body category because of the pivot required'
notes: Source frames paradox as body category — matches modern modifier_type='body'. Resolves paradox-tutorial.txt's '6th category' colloquial framing

subject: Pixie definition
source_doctrine: Source: 'quick inside-out dexterity directly following a set from a toe stall into another move'
modern_doctrine: Modern: pixie=+1 set modifier AND 2-ADD canonical trick
notes: Source's 'A pixie toe stall is the simplest form of this type of move' directly supports user's 2026-05-19 dual identity doctrine

subject: Fairy = reverse-direction pixie
source_doctrine: Source: 'A reverse direction Pixie set is called a Fairy'
modern_doctrine: Modern: fairy=+1 set modifier AND 2-ADD canonical trick
notes: Source establishes fairy as a direction-variant of pixie. Modern doctrine confirms via 4-primitive direction-quadrant matrix

subject: Pogo ADD value
source_doctrine: Source: pogo 'is usually worth 2 adds more than its non-pogo namesake' (= +1 dex + +1 body)
curator_action: surface as Red Wave 2 with reconciliation explanation
notes: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL FINDING: Source says pogo adds +2; modern pt5 says +0. Reconciliation via flag-count doctrine: modern pogo=+0 is the modifier-table incremental value (the +2 source-described is already counted via operational notation flags). NOT a true divergence but a counting-frame difference

subject: Slur = released-bag pogo
source_doctrine: Source: 'when this happens its a Slur which is only worth one add'
modern_doctrine: Modern: 'slur' NOT in modifier table OR canonical tricks
curator_action: surface as Red Wave 2: should slur be added as a +1 modifier or canonical trick?
notes: NEW CONCEPT: source defines slur as 'failed pogo' (bag not released before dex). Modern dictionary doesn't capture this concept

subject: Shooting = double-leg-pogo
source_doctrine: Source: shooting is like pogo but set goes under BOTH legs
notes: Source provides structural definition (under both legs); modern doctrine treats it as +3 set modifier without preserving the under-both-legs framing

subject: Stomping concept
source_doctrine: Source: 'planting both feet at once after setting the footbag then executing the desired move'
curator_action: surface as Red Wave 2: should stomping be added as a body modifier?
notes: NEW CONCEPT: source defines stomping as a distinct move element with a worked example (Stomping Double Leg-Over). Modern dictionary doesn't capture this

subject: Symposium definition
source_doctrine: Source: 'leg performing a dexterity is planted immediately before AND after the dexterity while the other (setting) leg does not touch the ground'
notes: Source provides PRECISE kinematic definition of symposium. Modern doctrine treats as +1 body modifier — aligned

subject: Down Diver Down trick
source_doctrine: Source: example trick = 'Down Diver Down' (same as Down Double Down except dive under bag before double dex)
notes: NEW CANONICAL CANDIDATE: source explicitly names this trick. Cross-references Job audit's 'Double Over Down' question
